mutations.change: Replace the MODEL operation with a PL/SQL nested loop that emits the same visible sample frames.
mutations.reason: T9.1 is explicitly an Oracle MODEL capability showcase, so output equality alone cannot waive the required database operation.

mutations.change: Generate the source rows and propagation rows in two separate MODEL clauses or SQL statements.
mutations.reason: The plan requires one documented MODEL-based operation for the whole animation, not staged cellular answers.

mutations.change: Use default or AUTOMATIC ORDER rules instead of the explicit RULES SEQUENTIAL ORDER contract.
mutations.reason: The cellular update ordering must be visible and stable rather than inferred from Oracle execution behavior.

mutations.change: Remove frame, y, or x from the explicit ordered rule evaluation dimensions.
mutations.reason: All three dimension directions are contractual and adversarial ordering probes distinguish their mutation.

mutations.change: Produce 120 frames after a full feasibility problem while leaving dimensions otherwise unchanged.
mutations.reason: The plan explicitly blocks on full-size failure and forbids reducing the required 150-frame animation.

mutations.change: Generate 128 columns and scale or pad them to the requested 160-column display width.
mutations.reason: The cellular grid itself must be full 160 by 96, not a resampled lower-resolution approximation.

mutations.change: Seed the base or propagation rule through DBMS_RANDOM, timestamp, session identifier, or client bytes.
mutations.reason: Independent exact frame hashes require the frozen coordinate-only noise function on every execution.

mutations.change: Add one to the coordinate noise polynomial before floor modulus for all generated cells.
mutations.reason: Exact independent cell recurrence and frame hashes detect even deterministic but unreviewed seed drift.

mutations.change: Set the complete source row to intensity 36 rather than the deterministic 28 through 36 base rule.
mutations.reason: A plausible bright source must still match every independently authored bottom-row coordinate.

mutations.change: Propagate only vertically and suppress the noise-selected minus-one, zero, or plus-one source x shift.
mutations.reason: Lateral stochastic motion is part of the exact cellular recurrence and the characteristic fire animation.

mutations.change: Copy the selected lower neighbor without subtracting the deterministic zero-through-two decay.
mutations.reason: The decay controls range and upward extinction and is asserted at every eligible destination cell.

mutations.change: Read the same y coordinate from the prior frame instead of the source cell one row below.
mutations.reason: The independently frozen neighbor direction is required for upward heat propagation.

mutations.change: Use Oracle MOD directly for wrapped negative x coordinates instead of the reviewed floor modulus.
mutations.reason: At the left edge a negative lateral source must wrap to column 159 and Oracle remainder semantics differ.

mutations.change: Hash or serialize column-major bytes while retaining row-major start offsets in run storage.
mutations.reason: Every digest is defined over exactly 15,360 unsigned bytes in y-major then x-major order.

mutations.change: Return or concatenate frames 74 and 75 in the opposite order while preserving individual hashes.
mutations.reason: The animation digest and dense frame numbers bind temporal order independently of per-frame content.

mutations.change: Merge equal intensities across an intervening cell or leave a one-offset gap between stored runs.
mutations.reason: Canonical decoding requires dense adjacent runs and byte-for-byte coverage of every offset exactly once.

mutations.change: Split one constant interval into adjacent equal-intensity runs that decode to identical bytes.
mutations.reason: Canonical compression forbids adjacent mergeable rows, preventing multiple encodings of one frame.

mutations.change: Persist one redundant JSON array or BLOB for each frame alongside otherwise valid run rows.
mutations.reason: The plan explicitly requires compact frame runs rather than redundant JSON or hidden decompressed answers.

mutations.change: Run the full operation immediately without first completing the documented 8 by 16 by 12 probe.
mutations.reason: Small feasibility evidence must establish the same operation shape before committing to full generation.

mutations.change: Insert the full frame rows and only then record a fabricated successful full feasibility probe.
mutations.reason: The explicit sequence and pre-insert empty-count evidence make feasibility a gate rather than a report.

mutations.change: Proceed after a conservative full estimate exceeds 256 MiB or omit runtime memory evidence.
mutations.reason: The required memory probe must be actionable and block work before destructive or high-risk insertion.

mutations.change: Commit each generated frame independently so a later failure leaves a plausible prefix in storage.
mutations.reason: A failed full-size operation blocks acceptance and must not replace the last complete accepted animation.

mutations.change: Include session identity or cursor execution count in the otherwise deterministic coordinate seed.
mutations.reason: Complete reruns in independent sessions must have identical run tuples and exact frame hashes.

mutations.change: Catch a full MODEL failure and retry fewer frames or smaller dimensions while reporting success.
mutations.reason: The charter explicitly says full-size failure blocks and dimensions or frame counts cannot be reduced.

mutations.change: Load expected hashes, fixture rules, or run tuples from evaluator or report files at generation time.
mutations.reason: Production must independently implement the contract and cannot couple to hidden evaluation answers.

mutations.change: Mark visual review approved using an evaluator-rendered preview before any accepted Oracle animation exists.
mutations.reason: Visual acceptance is a later human checkpoint over the real database-derived animation, never an invented golden.
